metar.name: Ozark/Cairns AAF, AL, US
metar.rawOb: METAR KOZR 130455Z AUTO 35006KT 10SM CLR 10/00 A3014 RMK AO2 SLP209 T01030003

sevenDay.synopsis: A line of storms blew thru quickly overnight, leaving MVFR skies and gusty winds in its wake. We'll see gusts up to 30kts during today's AM/PM periods, which could mean at least some LGT TURBC (Cat II a/c). Tomorrow and most of the weekend are smooth sailing. Overnight Sunday into Monday AM, another cold front will sweep thru, sparking showers and ISOLD TS. Behind the front on Monday, winds will once again be flirting with warning criteria (30kts). Once the front clears mid-AM, the rest of the forecast period should be VFR and hazard-free.
sevenDay.title: 7-DAY OUTLOOK: 12 MAR - 18 MAR
